source_snippet:
This is the FAaaS workstream to address critical security vulnerabilities from Mythos scan, resolve issues and assist in fleet rollout activities.

* FAaaS PMO ticket: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/browse/FAAASPMO-1263>
* Internal FAaaS slack chanel: [#internal-faaas-scan-kiwi](https://oracle.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C08PB5J87C5)
* External FAaaS slack channel with SaaS Security POCs: [#apps-division-scan-kiwi](https://oracle.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0AUEDVPU8K)

  


FAaaS Kiwi Workstreams
----------------------

### #1 Security scanning and fixing critical findings

1. [SaaS dashboard link](https://saasinsights.oraclecorp.com/ords/r/saaskpi/a1004/kiwi-saas-security-dashboard) - SaaS Security has scanned 97% (259 of 266) of FAaaS repos. Additional 28 FAaaSRM repos submitted for scanning
2. FAaaS Repos to scan: 266 FAaaS repos to provided SaaS security in new format: 
	1. Sampath has provided 28 additional FAaaS RM repos for FAAASSRE Jira and these are submitted for scanning - [devops-coderepository link](https://devops.oci.oraclecorp.com/devops-coderepository/projects/ocid1.devopsproject.oc1.phx.amaaaaaaw4vcxbyalo5gavleayvmmkytburb5xxytnb5ysd3ltvnwdryqztq/repositories?_ctx=us-phoenix-1%2Cdevops_scm_central)
3. FAaaS Jiras:
	1. FAaaS Kiwi JIRA dashboard: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/secure/Dashboard.jspa?selectPageId=73429>
	2. FAaaS Kiwi JIRA filter: <https://jira.oci.oraclecorp.com/issues/?filter=219921>
4. Working group with engineers to accelerate the issue triage and resolution process. Sunil hosted on Monday 5/18 with ADP: Sang, Norman, FACP: Matthias, Junren, HCP: Arup, Abhay, Fang

### #2 Fleet Patching

1. Leadership ask is to come up with a proposal for monthly full fleet patching in June and beyond. Options are under review (**Siju**, **Venkatesh**) - [Patching proposal image](https://confluence.oraclecorp.com/confluence/download/attachments/20352556426/image-2026-5-7_18-0-13.png?version=1&modificationDate=1778191217000&api=v2)
2. Patching proposal reviewed on 5/12 Strategic Ops -
3. From Preeti, for the June tactical + future support for fleetwide patching, there are some ERs we need filed / tracked as part of the program as far as Refresh goes:
	1. We need to support bulk cancel API to get pre-existing conflicting schedules out of the way.
	2. We need to get a finalized blurb for customer notification assuming we want to notify the customer why their Refresh was canceled.

### #3 DB Patching

1. One round of Ksplice patching done:
	1. PROD Clusters: Total: 854/855 Completed, Pending: 1 (eiaa1a030 - Inactive cluster . Nothing to patch)
	2. Non-PROD Clusters (Pre-Prod, PINT, Beta): All Completed (209/209)
	3. DEMO Clusters: All Completed (93/93)
2. For non-Ksplice patching, Currently only 2 slots of 3 days each are available per month. In order to do full fleet, Sravan needs 5 slots of 3 days each
3. We need clarity from leadership if DB patching every month is a requirement and exactly what types of patching (DomU, Dom0, GI)

### #4 OS Patching

1. Ed is working with Erna's team on OS patching of Linux image

Appendix - Supporting Materials
-------------------------------

FAaaS repo list: <https://confluence.oraclecorp.com/confluence/display/OCIFACP/FAaaS+Repos+for+Security+Scanning>

SaaS Kiwi dashboard: [https://saasinsights.oraclecorp.com/ords/r/saaskpi/a1004/kiwi-saas-security-dashboard](https://oracle-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/mauruthi_geetha_mohan_oracle_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BC3BB40F4-2031-48A1-B6E0-353419DCCEB7%7D&file=kiwi-priority.xlsx&wdLOR=c1E39D1D1-9F92-0444-A12F-1DA0E11A44AA&nav=MTVfe0IwQzdFNERGLUYzNjUtNEM3Ri1BQTA3LTVCMDREMTgxNkI4Q30&action=default&mobileredirect=true)

SaaS Kiwi xls: [kiwi-priority.xlsx](https://oracle-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/personal/mauruthi_geetha_mohan_oracle_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7BC3BB40F4-2031-48A1-B6E0-353419DCCEB7%7D&file=kiwi-priority.xlsx&wdLOR=c1E39D1D1-9F92-0444-A12F-1DA0E11A44AA&nav=MTVfe0IwQzdFNERGLUYzNjUtNEM3Ri1BQTA3LTVCMDREMTgxNkI4Q30&action=default&mobileredirect=true)

expected_extraction.project_purpose: Address critical security vulnerabilities identified by Mythos scanning by driving repository scanning, fixing critical findings, and supporting fleet rollout and patching activities for FAaaS.
expected_extraction.rag_summary: Amber — Scanning and Ksplice patching are near-complete, but monthly fleet/DB patching approach and capacity (non-Ksplice slots) plus leadership decisions are gating sustained progress.
expected_extraction.schedule_health: At risk — schedule baseline not stated; progress depends on leadership decisions for monthly fleet/DB patching and securing non-Ksplice patching capacity.
metrics_snapshot: Additional repos submitted for scanning: 28 FAaaSRM repos
Non-Ksplice patching capacity vs need: 2 slots/month available vs 5 needed (3 days each)
workstreams_summary: #1 Security scanning — 97% of repos scanned with a working group accelerating triage and critical issue resolution; remaining repos and newly submitted RM repos are in the scanning pipeline.
#2 Fleet patching — Monthly full-fleet patching proposal for June+ is under review, with leadership alignment and required Refresh-related ERs needed to enable execution.

#3 DB patching — Ksplice patching is essentially complete, but non-Ksplice capacity is insufficient and leadership clarity is needed on whether monthly DB patching is required and what patch types are in scope.
#4 OS patching — Linux image OS patching is in progress in collaboration with Erna’s team.

blocking_issues.description: Leadership decision needed on whether DB patching every month is required and what patching types are in scope (DomU, Dom0, GI).
Non-Ksplice DB patching capacity shortfall: only 2 slots/month available vs 5 slots/month needed (3 days each) to cover full fleet.
Monthly full fleet patching proposal for June and beyond requires leadership approval; options under review by Siju and Venkatesh.
Refresh enablement ERs must be filed/tracked (bulk cancel API; finalized customer notification blurb) to support June tactical and future fleetwide patching.

next_steps: Finalize and socialize monthly full-fleet patching proposal (June+) — this sprint
File and begin tracking Refresh ERs (bulk cancel API; customer notification blurb) — this sprint
Confirm DB monthly patching requirement and patching scope (DomU/Dom0/GI) — this sprint
Plan to close remaining repo scanning gap and drive critical finding remediation via working group cadence — this sprint
exec_asks: Approve monthly full-fleet patching approach — enables June+ predictable patch cadence.
Decide if monthly DB patching is required — unlocks planning and resourcing.
Confirm DB patching scope (DomU/Dom0/GI) — prevents rework and missed coverage.
Support securing additional non-Ksplice patching slots — required to patch full fleet.
decisions_needed: Whether monthly full fleet patching will be executed starting June and beyond.
Whether DB patching every month is a requirement.
Which DB patching types are in scope (DomU, Dom0, GI).
How to close the non-Ksplice patching capacity gap (2 slots/month vs 5 needed).
capacity_constraints: Non-Ksplice patching capacity: 2 slots/month available vs 5 needed (3 days each)
dependencies_external_teams: SaaS Security POCs — scanning coverage and findings triage
Erna’s team — OS patching for Linux image
ADP/FACP/HCP engineering reps — working group for issue resolution
